metadata.name: unus-data
metadata.summary: A package to handle protein datacard for the protein project

functions.name: unus_data.data.parsers.parse_fasta
documentation:
Parses FASTA string and returns list of strings with amino-acid sequences.
Arguments:
  fasta_string: The string contents of a FASTA file.

Returns:
  A tuple of two lists:
  * A list of sequences.
  * A list of sequence descriptions taken from the comment lines. In the
    same order as the sequences.

functions.name: unus_data.data.parsers.parse_uniprot_fasta_desc
documentation: Parses the description string in a Uniprot FASTA file and return lists of strings

functions.name: unus_data.data.parsers.parse_dssp
documentation: Parses DSSP output and returns a dictionary. Note that the orginal PDB can contain missing residues, the length of the sequence is not always equal to the length of the DSSP output. Therefore, complete length is passed as an optional input to fill the missing residues with 'X'; Otherwise, we will use the length of the DSSP output as the length of the sequence.

functions.name: unus_data.data.protein.from_pdb_string
documentation:
Takes a PDB string and constructs a Protein object.

WARNING: All non-standard residue types will be converted into UNK. All
  non-standard atoms will be ignored.

Args:
  pdb_str: The contents of the pdb file
  chain_id: If chain_id is specified (e.g. A), then only that chain
    is parsed. Otherwise all chains are parsed.

Returns:
  A new `Protein` parsed from the pdb contents.

functions.name: unus_data.data.protein.to_pdb
documentation:
Converts a `Protein` instance to a PDB string.

Args:
  prot: The protein to convert to PDB.

Returns:
  PDB string.

functions.name: unus_data.data.protein.ideal_atom_mask
documentation:
Computes an ideal atom mask.

`Protein.atom_mask` typically is defined according to the atoms that are
reported in the PDB. This function computes a mask according to heavy atoms
that should be present in the given sequence of amino acids.

Args:
  prot: `Protein` whose fields are `numpy.ndarray` objects.

Returns:
  An ideal atom mask.

documentation: Tool wrapper for running ProteinMPNN model ...

methods.name: unus_data.tools.dssp.DSSP.run_dir

Python wrapper of DSSP. 
*Install command: `sudo apt-get install dssp`

methods.name: unus_data.tools.foldseek.FoldSeek.cluster
documentation: Run easy-cluster algorithm. It accepts input in either PDB or MMCIF format, with support for both flat and gzipped files.

functions.name: unus_data.data.residue_constants.load_stereo_chemical_props
documentation:
Load stereo_chemical_props.txt into a nice structure.

Load literature values for bond lengths and bond angles and translate
bond angles into the length of the opposite edge of the triangle
("residue_virtual_bonds").

Returns:
  residue_bonds: Dict that maps resname -> list of Bond tuples.
  residue_virtual_bonds: Dict that maps resname -> list of Bond tuples.
  residue_bond_angles: Dict that maps resname -> list of BondAngle tuples.

functions.name: unus_data.data.residue_constants.sequence_to_onehot
documentation:
Maps the given sequence into a one-hot encoded matrix.

Args:
  sequence: An amino acid sequence.
  mapping: A dictionary mapping amino acids to integers.
  map_unknown_to_x: If True, any amino acid that is not in the mapping will be
    mapped to the unknown amino acid 'X'. If the mapping doesn't contain
    amino acid 'X', an error will be thrown. If False, any amino acid not in
    the mapping will throw an error.

Returns:
  A numpy array of shape (seq_len, num_unique_aas) with one-hot encoding of
  the sequence.

Raises:
  ValueError: If the mapping doesn't contain values from 0 to
    num_unique_aas - 1 without any gaps.

functions.name: unus_data.data.residue_constants.chi_angle_atom
documentation: Define chi-angle rigid groups via one-hot representations.
A one hot representation for the first and second atoms defining the axis
of rotation for each chi-angle in each residue.
